explanation: The user command 'turn around' means the robot should rotate 180 degrees to face the opposite direction. Since the robot starts facing a certain direction, turning around requires two rotations of 90 degrees in opposite directions (e.g., RotateLeft twice) to achieve a full 180-degree turn. This is a standard interpretation of 'turn around' in a robot context. As there are no objects involved or any state constraints, the plan consists of two Motion(RotateLeft) actions to achieve the desired orientation.
